Cash App owner Block pays $40 million fine to New York over compliance failures
data.description:
Block will pay a $40 million civil fine and hire an independent monitor to settle charges by New York's financial services regulator that it failed to adequately police and stop money laundering on its Cash App mobile payment service.

Announcing the fine on Thursday, New York's Department of Financial Services faulted "critical gaps" in the Bank Secrecy Act, anti-money laundering and know-your-customer programs at Block, led by Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey and formerly known as Square.

Block agreed in January to pay an $80 million civil fine to settle similar charges by 48 U.S. state financial regulators.

In a statement, the Oakland, California-based payments company did not admit or deny wrongdoing, and said the New York settlement ends "all previously pending state money transmission license matters."

Apps such as Cash App and Venmo make it easy for people to transmit money.

The New York regulator said Block's alleged shortfalls included inadequate customer due diligence, and inadequate risk-based controls to counter illegal activity such as money laundering and terrorism financing.

It also said Block's lax oversight of bitcoin transactions, which it began offering through Cash App in 2018, and the company's subsequent rapid growth created "an environment vulnerable to criminal exploitation."

The regulator cited Block's discovery in a 2022 internal investigation of 8,359 Cash App accounts linked to a Russian criminal network.

Adrienne Harris, New York's superintendent of financial services, said compliance functions "must keep pace with company growth or expansion" at both traditional financial services companies and emerging cryptocurrency platforms.

Cash App had $283 billion of inflows in 2024, and 57 million monthly users at year end, a regulatory filing shows.

data.title: Croatia's regional development minister resigning to run for mayor
data.description:
ZAGREB (Croatia), April 10 (SeeNews) - The Croatian minister of regional development and European Union funds Sime Erlic would step down as of Friday to run for a mayor of the Adriatic city of Zadar, prime minister Andrej Plenkovic said on Thursday.

"We wish you success in your new challenge, the race for mayor of Zadar," Plenkovic told Erlic during a weekly cabinet meeting as seen in a video published on the website of public television HRT.

Erlic has served as minister of regional development and EU funds for more than two years, after serving as state secretary at the same ministry.

As of Saturday, the current state secretary Zrinka Raguz will become acting minister of regional development.

Croatia will hold local elections on May 18.

UAB “Orkela”, legal entity code 304099538, registered address Jogailos g. 4, Vilnius, Republic of Lithuania (hereinafter – the Issuer), whose securities (hereinafter – the Bonds) are included and traded on the Nasdaq bond list, and which are also publicly offered under the base prospectus approved by the Bank of Lithuania on 14 November 2023 (hereinafter – the Prospectus), hereby informs of the following:

The Issuer announces that a building completion certificate has been issued for the buildings developed under the Issuer’s project located at Vasario 16-osios g. 1, with  adresses  Vasario 16-osios g. 1, Vasario 16-osios g. 3, Vasario 16-osios g. 7 and Vasario 16-osios g. 9 in Vilnius. This marks an important milestone in the implementation of the project and enables the use of the buildings according to their intended purpose.

It should be noted that the building completion certificate for the hotel building at Vasario 16-osios g. 5, Vilnius , which is also part of the same project, is expected in the summer. Fit-out works are currently ongoing in the hotel building.

Director

Anastasija Pocienė

data.title: FLUENCE ENERGY SHAREHOLDER ALERT: CLAIMSFILER REMINDS INVESTORS WITH LOSSES IN EXCESS OF $100,000 of Lead Plaintiff Deadline in Class Action Lawsuit Against Fluence Energy, Inc. - FLNC
data.description:
NEW ORLEANS, April 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — ClaimsFiler, a FREE shareholder information service, reminds investors that they have until May 12, 2025 to file lead plaintiff applications in a securities class action lawsuit against Fluence Energy, Inc. (NasdaqGS: FLNC), if they purchased the Company’s shares between November 29, 2023, and February 10, 2025, inclusive (the “Class Period”). This action is pending in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia.

Get Help

Fluence investors should visit us at https://claimsfiler.com/cases/nasdaq-flnc/ or call toll-free (844) 367-9658. Lawyers at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC are available to discuss your legal options.

About the Lawsuit

Fluence and certain of its executives are charged with failing to disclose material information during the Class Period, violating federal securities laws.

On February 10, 2025, the Company announced its financial results for 1Q 2025, disclosing a net loss of $57 million, or $0.32 per share, compared to a loss of $25.6 million, or $0.14 per share, for the same period in the prior year, revenues down 49% year-over-year to $186.8 million, and decreased revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025, to a range of $3.1 billion to $3.7 billion, from its prior outlook of $3.6 billion to $4.4 billion, due to “customer-driven delays in signing certain contracts that, coupled with competitive pressures, result in the need to lower our fiscal year 2025 outlook.”

On this news, the price of Fluence’s shares fell $6.07 per share, or 46.44%, to close at $7.00 per share on February 11, 2025.

The case is Daniel Abramov v. Fluence Energy, Inc., et al., No. 25-cv-00444.

About ClaimsFiler

ClaimsFiler has a single mission: to serve as the information source to help retail investors recover their share of billions of dollars from securities class action settlements. At ClaimsFiler.com, investors can: (1) register for free to gain access to information and settlement websites for various securities class action cases so they can timely submit their own claims; (2) upload their portfolio transactional data to be notified about relevant securities cases in which they may have a financial interest; and (3) submit inquiries to the Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC law firm for free case evaluations.

To learn more about ClaimsFiler, visit www.claimsfiler.com.

data.title: E.L.F. BEAUTY SHAREHOLDER ALERT: CLAIMSFILER REMINDS INVESTORS WITH LOSSES IN EXCESS OF $100,000 of Lead Plaintiff Deadline in Class Action Lawsuit Against e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. - ELF
data.description:
NEW ORLEANS, April 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — ClaimsFiler, a FREE shareholder information service, reminds investors that they have until May 5, 2025 to file lead plaintiff applications in a securities class action lawsuit against e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. (“ELF” or the “Company”) , if they purchased the Company’s securities between November 1, 2023 and November 19, 2024, inclusive (the “Class Period”). This action is pending in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California.

Get Help

ELF investors should visit us at https://claimsfiler.com/cases/nyse-elf/ or call toll-free (844) 367-9658. Lawyers at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC are available to discuss your legal options.

About the Lawsuit

ELF and certain of its executives are charged with failing to disclose material information during the Class Period, violating federal securities laws.

On November 20, 2024, Muddy Waters Research reported that the Company had materially overstated revenue over the past three quarters; that in Q2 FY24, it realized its growth narrative was in trouble as its inventory built; that it then began reporting inflated revenue and profits resulting in its reported inventory also appearing materially inflated; and that the Company concealed its inventory challenges from investors by falsely attributing its rising inventory levels to supposed changes in its sourcing practices rather than the true cause insufficient sales.

On this news, the price of ELF’s shares fell $2.71 per share, or 2.23%, to close at $119.00 per share on November 20, 2024.

On February 6, 2025, the Company released its fiscal Q3 2025 results and provided fiscal 2025 outlook that confirmed the weaknesses identified in the report previously issued by Muddy Waters, including softer consumption trends and slower new product launches. On this news, Elf’s stock price fell $17.36 per share, or 19.62%, to close at $71.13 per share on February 7, 2025.

The case is Rottman v. e.l.f. Beauty, Inc., et al., No. 25-cv-2316.

About ClaimsFiler

ClaimsFiler has a single mission: to serve as the information source to help retail investors recover their share of billions of dollars from securities class action settlements. At ClaimsFiler.com, investors can: (1) register for free to gain access to information and settlement websites for various securities class action cases so they can timely submit their own claims; (2) upload their portfolio transactional data to be notified about relevant securities cases in which they may have a financial interest; and (3) submit inquiries to the Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC law firm for free case evaluations.

To learn more about ClaimsFiler, visit www.claimsfiler.com.

data.title: FBN INTERVIEW WITH BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 10, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY

FBN INTERVIEW WITH BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

FBN INTERVIEW WITH BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY

APRIL 10, 2025

SPEAKERS:

BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY

STUART VARNEY, FBN HOST

STUART VARNEY, FOX BUSINESS HOST: Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins joins me now.

Madam Secretary, the farmers tell us they need financial help. How much are you going to give them?

BROOKE ROLLINS, U.S. AGRICULTURE SECRETARY: Well, good morning, Stuart. It's great to be with you. Clearly, we are in a realignment of the American economy phase. President Trump has been resolute and convicted in his principle of ensuring that America is put first always, whether it's the toolkit, including tariffs, whether it's deregulation, the tax cuts that the Hill is now moving toward and all of the above approach.

But to your question, and as secretary of agriculture, I am hyper-focused on the effects to our farmers and our ranchers. Under four years of Joe Biden, the cost of input went up 30 percent. We had a trade deficit of $49 billion, meaning our product wasn't moving out the way that it was under Trump won. And at the end of the day, we don't know exactly what effect will be on our farmers and our ranchers in the short term.

We do know the effect in the long term that the prosperity, that the golden age of farming and ranching, but, frankly, the entire American economy is coming. We're obviously very convicted in that idea, But as we move forward and into the next few months and into planting season and how this is going to affect our farmers, especially, our row croppers, especially, USDA will, just as we did under the first term with President Trump, under Secretary Sonny Perdue, ensure that our farmers are protected as the conversations continue.

VARNEY: So, you'll give him some money. I got it.

ROLLINS: Well, we'll see. Yes, to be determined. But, hopefully, we won't need to Stuart, right? Hopefully.

VARNEY: That would be good.

ROLLINS: We saw some great movement yesterday. We'll continue to see it. We've got 75 countries here at the White House going into a cabinet meeting right after I finish with you to talk about it. So, we're making the right moves and hopefully that money will not be necessary.

VARNEY: Let me change the subject for a moment. According to the CDC, 3,000 people die each year, 48 million or sickened from food borne illnesses. Do you have a plan to prevent more outbreaks?

ROLLINS: Well, every day at USDA, and, obviously, I think one of the incredible difference makers that is this cabinet and is this president is, this is our fourth cabinet meeting in about six or seven weeks, which is sort of amazing, is that we are working so closely together.

So, the food borne illness is certainly a challenge, but I've got Bobby Kennedy, my great partner over at HHS, of course, the incredible leaders that are moving into the CDC and NIH, lots of three letter acronyms here, working alongside at USDA with our team. So, yes, we will be addressing that and already are addressing it every day with our credible scientists and experts we have.

VARNEY: Madam Secretary, President Trump signed an executive order opening up, what, 50 percent of protected forest land for logging. He wants to increase domestic production. I'm a private tree farmer. Not all of us are happy about this competition that we're getting from the feds. Any comment?

ROLLINS: Well, first of all, let me say this, that America should be producing our own timber for our own people instead over many years, mainly because of the lefts and the Democrats' agenda on the, quote, environment protection, which in fact just basically is destroying our forest because we can't get in there and clean them out. We're going to start doing that now and have already started.

But that we are having to rely too much on import of timber for what we need to do and build in America, and that is not okay. It is more than just a cost of goods and honoring our farmers and making sure that our own people can use our own timber, but it's a national security issue, whether it's timber or food or energy, whatever it is, if we're relying more on other countries, that's not what's best for America.

So, opening up the forest lands and in a percentage of those, ensuring that we're moving permitting more quickly, ensuring we're deregulating will bring more prosperity, not just in the cost of the good, but also to rural parts of America, more jobs, better jobs, and we're really excited about that. The president's vision is right on, and we've been implementing it at USDA.

VARNEY: Out of time. Secretary Rollins, thank you very much for being with us today, a pleasure.

ROLLINS: I love being with you, Stuart. Thank you so much.

VARNEY: You got it.

END

data.title: ARA oil product stocks fall 3%, Insights Global data shows
data.description:
Oil product stocks independently held in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) refining and storage hub fell by more than 3% on the week, data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global showed on Thursday.

  • Total stocks stood at 5.89 million metric tons, the data showed.

  • Gasoline stocks fell 2.3% to 1.43 million tons on higher transatlantic exports, Insight Global's Lars van Wageningen said.

  • Gasoil stocks, which include diesel, were down 4.4% at 2.06 million tons as flows to inland locations increased, Wageningen said.

  • Fuel oil stocks rose 4% to 1.07 million tons on weaker bunker fuel demand and slower exports, Wageningen said.

data.title: AquaSculpt Reviews (BREAKING): 27,000 CONSUMERS Tracked Over 90 Days—We Finally Have the Answer
data.description:
TALLMADGE, Ohio, April 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Is the viral “Ice Water Hack” truly a revolutionary breakthrough in weight loss—or just another internet fad?

With AquaSculpt dominating TikTok, wellness blogs, and influencer reels, we set out to find out whether this increasingly popular supplement actually lives up to the hype. After Tracking 27,000 Verified Aquasculpt Users, analyzing customer reviews, compiling expert commentary, and reviewing usage patterns over 30, 60, and 90-day timeframes, we’ve built what might be the most comprehensive consumer-led report on AquaSculpt to date.

This isn’t a personal anecdote. It’s a data-driven breakdown of real-world outcomes. Our goal? To separate viral buzz from verifiable results—and uncover whether AquaSculpt’s bold claims hold up under scrutiny.

AquaSculpt claims to turn the body into a 24/7 fat-burning machine by activating thermogenesis through a Morning Ritual Known As The 7-Second Ice Water Hack. Rather than relying on stimulants or crash diets, it leverages a blend of metabolism-supporting nutrients to trigger sustained fat oxidation with just a glass of cold water and one capsule per day.

But does the data actually support these claims? And are the transformations shown online typical—or exceptions?

We compiled results from verified testimonials, tracked user-reported outcomes, consulted ingredient scientists, and analyzed feedback across TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and wellness forums.

>> What If Aquasculpt Is The Missing Link For Your Stubborn Fat Loss? You’ll Never Know Unless You Try.

Here’s what we found…

Why AquaSculpt Is Suddenly Everywhere – A Quick Overview

AquaSculpt’s rise wasn’t a slow burn—it was a digital explosion.

The product first gained traction on TikTok, where viral videos labeled “#IceWaterHack” started pulling in millions of views. Users claimed to be melting pounds by doing nothing more than taking a single capsule each morning with a glass of ice water. No workout montages. No diet plans. Just visible transformation—and a shared link to The Official Aquasculpt Website.

From there, the momentum snowballed. Mentions of AquaSculpt started appearing in:

  • Weight loss and metabolism-focused Reddit threads
  • Facebook support groups for women over 40
  • Instagram transformation reels from lifestyle influencers
  • YouTube testimonials titled “How I Lost 40lbs in 60 Days WITHOUT Dieting”

So what exactly is driving the obsession?

At the center of it all is AquaSculpt’s 7-second ritual—a method combining cold exposure with a synergistic blend of thermogenic nutrients that claims to extend your body’s fat-burning window far beyond traditional exercise.

In an era where consumers are exhausted by stimulant-heavy supplements, fad diets, and one-size-fits-all solutions, AquaSculpt’s simplicity and science-first approach struck a nerve.

And based on our data, the curiosity is translating into commitment. Over 81% Of Users Who Started With A 30-Day Supply Went On To Reorder Within Two Months (Official)—a strong signal that people aren’t just trying AquaSculpt… they’re sticking with it.

What Makes AquaSculpt So Unique? (And Why It’s Not Just Another Weight Loss Supplement)

The wellness industry is saturated with “solutions” that offer short-lived results—powders that bloat you, pills that spike your heart rate, and teas that drain water weight without addressing real fat stores.

AquaSculpt flips that script.

Instead of masking hunger or forcing unsustainable calorie cuts, it targets the root problem many struggling with weight face: a metabolism that’s “turned off” for most of the day. According to the brand and backed by user feedback, AquaSculpt works by reactivating dormant metabolic pathways—specifically through a blend of cold-activated ingredients designed to stimulate thermogenesis, a process where the body burns calories to generate heat.

What makes it so different?

  • Zero stimulants – No caffeine crash. No racing heart. Just clean, sustained energy.
  • No strict food rules – Works with real-world lifestyles, not against them.
  • Real science behind the formula – AquaSculpt contains compounds like CGA, EGCG, L-Carnitine, and Chromium—each with research-backed benefits tied to fat burning, blood sugar regulation, and energy metabolism.

Unlike most thermogenics that wear off in under 30 minutes, Aquasculpt Is Designed To Prolong Fat Oxidation For Hours After Use—especially when activated by cold water. It mimics the effects of an ice bath or cold plunge, without the discomfort, while using natural botanicals to keep the metabolic “switch” on longer.

It’s a small habit that claims to deliver big returns. But as you’ll soon see, user data may prove those claims are more than marketing.

>> I Grabbed Aquasculpt During The Limited Launch—75% Off, No Autoship Traps, No Gimmicks.

Who’s Really Using AquaSculpt? We Analyzed the Demographics Behind the Ice Water Hack Craze

When AquaSculpt first gained traction on social media, the assumption was that it was just another passing trend for 20-something influencers. But when we analyzed 27,000+ AquaSculpt users across forums, review platforms, and survey submissions, a much broader, more diverse picture emerged.

The truth is—AquaSculpt reviews come from every demographic you can imagine, and the one thing they all have in common is this: they were tired of doing everything “right” and still seeing no progress on the scale.

The Typical AquaSculpt User (Based on 27,000 Profiles):

  • Age Range: 66% of AquaSculpt users fall between 35 and 64 years old, with a significant portion (22%) over 50. This product isn’t just for young gym-goers—it’s for people looking to reignite a sluggish metabolism, especially those in midlife and post-menopause stages.
  • Gender: While 72% Of Aquasculpt Reviews Come From Women, The Male User Base Is Growing Quickly (Verified Source), particularly among men over 40 who are experiencing slowed fat-burning and reduced energy.
  • Common Lifestyles: Most users were not “fitness fanatics.” In fact, the majority had sedentary jobs, high stress, or family obligations that limited their time and energy for intense workouts.
  • Health Goals: Based on AquaSculpt reviews and survey insights, the primary goals reported include breaking through stubborn weight plateaus, increasing daily energy, curbing sugar cravings, and reclaiming confidence without extreme dieting.

Many of the Most Positive Aquasculpt Reviews Came From Users Who Had Tried Multiple Weight-Loss Products In The Past, but found that AquaSculpt was the first one that felt realistic, sustainable, and science-based.

If you’ve ever struggled with:

  • Weight gain during or after menopause
  • Stress eating and blood sugar crashes
  • Lack of time or energy for structured workouts
  • Trying countless fat burners with no real results

...then chances are, you’re exactly who AquaSculpt was made for.

And the growing wave of AquaSculpt reviews from real-world users across the U.S. proves this product isn’t niche—it’s a new standard for sustainable fat-burning.

>> AQUASCULPT EITHER DELIVERS SCULPTED, LEANER RESULTS—OR YOU GET A FULL REFUND. NO RISK.

How Fast Do Real Users See Results? AquaSculpt Reviews Reveal the 30, 60, and 90-Day Transformation Curve

If you’re like most readers scouring AquaSculpt reviews, one burning question is likely top of mind:

“How fast will I actually see results?”

To answer that, we compiled data from thousands of AquaSculpt users who followed the 7-second Ice Water Hack consistently. We tracked results at Month 1, Month 2, and Month 3—and the trends were shockingly consistent.

Month 1: Early Wins That Hooked Users

Within the first 7–10 days, many AquaSculpt reviews mention a “subtle but powerful shift.”

  • Appetite regulation is the #1 early win—users report feeling less hungry between meals, fewer cravings, and an ability to say no to sweets without effort.
  • Energy levels noticeably improve, especially in the mid-morning and mid-afternoon slumps.
  • Bloating and digestion normalize quickly, with many claiming their stomach looks flatter after dinner, not puffier. On average, users lost between 4–7 pounds in the first 30 days—without changing their diets drastically.

>> You’ve Got 60 Full Days To Try AquaSculpt And See Results—Or Your Money Back.

Month 2: Real Momentum Begins

In the second month, the AquaSculpt formula begins to compound in its effects.

  • Fat loss accelerates, especially in the belly, thigh, and hip regions.
  • Most AquaSculpt reviews mention clothes fitting differently, confidence improving, and energy sustaining into the evenings.
  • A Growing Number Of Users Reported Better Sleep, Mood Stability, and fewer emotional eating triggers.
  • Average fat loss during this window climbed to 8–12 pounds total by Day 60 for consistent users.

Month 3: Long-Term Metabolic Reset Kicks In

By the 90-day mark, most AquaSculpt users reported a complete shift in how their body responds to food and energy.

  • The metabolism no longer feels “off” or sluggish.
  • Weight continues to drop without added effort, with some losing 15–25+ lbs total.
  • Multiple AquaSculpt reviews described it as “the first product I’ve stuck with for more than 3 months—because it’s actually working.”

We also found that Users Who Paired Aquasculpt With Basic Hydration And Light Daily Walking saw results accelerate even more—without intense workouts.

How Much Does AquaSculpt Cost? (And Which Bundle Delivers the Best Results?)

Let’s get real—if you’ve read through dozens of AquaSculpt reviews, you’re probably wondering whether it’s worth the price tag. We broke down every bundle, bonus, and value add offered on the Official Aquasculpt Website—and here’s what you should know.

AquaSculpt Pricing Breakdown (Updated for 2025):

  • 1 Bottle (30-Day Supply) – $69 + shipping Best for first-time users testing AquaSculpt for 1 month. But at $2.30 per serving, this is the least cost-efficient option.
  • 3 Bottles (90-Day Supply) – $177 total ($59/bottle) Most popular starter bundle. Includes free shipping, a free bonus guide, and enough supply to track full-body results over the recommended 90-day usage window.
  • 6 Bottles (180-Day Supply) – $234 total ($39/bottle) Best value. Comes with:
    • 2 Free Bonus eBooks
    • VIP Support Access
    • Free Fast Shipping
    • $660+ in savings compared to buying monthly

If you're serious about resetting your metabolism with AquaSculpt, this bundle delivers the best ROI and ensures you won’t run out before results peak.

What About Refunds and Guarantees?

All orders come with a 60-day money-back guarantee—no questions asked. So if AquaSculpt doesn’t deliver visible results, you’re fully protected.

One Warning: Avoid 3rd-Party Sellers

There have been multiple fake AquaSculpt listings on Amazon, eBay, and shady sites. These knockoff versions do not contain the real Ice Water Hack formula and are not covered by the refund policy.

To protect yourself: Order directly from the Official Aquasculpt Site Only.

The Most Common AquaSculpt Success Stories – 6 Real User Profiles That Reveal What’s Actually Possible

As we analyzed over 27,000 AquaSculpt reviews and testimonials, a pattern started to emerge. While the results varied, six major “user profiles” came up again and again—each showing a different kind of transformation that proves AquaSculpt’s real-world potential.

These aren’t influencers or actors. These are real, everyday people who decided to give the Ice Water Hack and AquaSculpt formula a shot—and watched their bodies (and lives) change from the inside out.

1. Paula S. – 47, Albany, NY

“I dropped 54 pounds and six dress sizes—and never once gave up my favorite meals.”

A mom of three struggling with hormonal weight gain, Paula tried fasting, cardio, and calorie-counting with little to show for it. After just 3 weeks on AquaSculpt, her sugar cravings disappeared. By Month 3, she had lost over 50 pounds—without ever going to the gym. “I don’t feel like the ‘bigger friend’ anymore,” she shared in one of the top AquaSculpt reviews on Facebook.

2. James R. – 56, Austin, TX

“I lost 28 pounds—and I wasn’t even trying that hard.”

James is a full-time software engineer with a sedentary routine. He started AquaSculpt as a skeptic but stuck to the 7-second Ice Water Hack. He now reports better digestion, more stamina, and significantly less joint pain. “I used to avoid mirrors. Now I’m seeing abs I haven’t seen since college.”

>> I Didn’t Expect AquaSculpt To Actually—But Two Months Later, I Was Down Two Sizes (75% Off).

3. Michelle D. – 34, New York City, NY

“I used to be the exhausted one in the group—now I’m the one with energy.”

Michelle battled sugar crashes and mental fog for years. With AquaSculpt, she felt more focused and balanced by Day 10. Her weight dropped steadily (38 pounds total), but her mood, confidence, and ability to sleep deeply were what stood out the most. Her viral TikTok AquaSculpt review has inspired thousands to try the hack themselves.

4. Maria H. – 61, Tampa, FL

“Menopause made me feel like nothing would work again. AquaSculpt proved me wrong.”

After gaining 40 pounds post-menopause, Maria said she was “ready to give up.” AquaSculpt helped her reset her sluggish metabolism—without starving herself. Now 31 pounds down, she says she feels “15 years younger.” Her AquaSculpt review is one of the top-rated on Amazon, even though the brand encourages purchasing through the official site only.

5. Keisha B. – 39, Atlanta, GA

“I thought I needed a trainer… turns out I just needed AquaSculpt.”

Keisha struggled with self-esteem after years of failed gym routines and diet apps. AquaSculpt gave her a simple daily ritual that made her feel in control again. In 90 days, she shed 42 pounds and says her energy “never dipped once.” Her Instagram AquaSculpt reviews are now followed by thousands.

6. Larry G. – 50, Denver, CO

“My wife and I both took it. I lost 23 pounds. She lost 31. We Feel Like We Got Our Marriage Back.”

Larry’s story was the most emotional. He and his wife committed to AquaSculpt together. Not only did they lose a combined 54 pounds, but they said it helped reignite their relationship. “It gave us back our energy, our spark—and our health.”

These are just a handful of the Aquasculpt Reviews We Encountered—but they reflect a very real trend: when users stay consistent, they often gain far more than just weight loss.

Surprising Benefits We Didn’t Expect to Hear from AquaSculpt Users—Beyond Just Fat Loss

While most AquaSculpt reviews focus on weight loss, hundreds of users began reporting unexpected improvements—the kind of benefits that weren’t promised on the label but showed up anyway. These are the kinds of perks that turn customers into lifelong fans.

Mental Clarity and Focus

“I feel like my brain isn’t foggy anymore.”

Multiple AquaSculpt users reported sharper concentration, improved short-term memory, and the ability to work longer without zoning out.

Deeper, More Restorative Sleep

“I don’t toss and turn like I used to.”

One of the most repeated bonus benefits in AquaSculpt reviews was improved sleep quality. As blood sugar levels and cortisol stabilized, people reported falling asleep faster and waking up refreshed.

Less Stress-Eating and Emotional Snacking

“It’s like my cravings vanished.”

AquaSculpt wasn’t marketed as an emotional eating solution—but countless reviewers say they found themselves saying no to cookies and chips without effort or guilt.

>>That’s Exactly What Aquasculpt Does (Proof Inside).

Healthier-Looking Skin

“I didn’t change my skincare, but my face is glowing.”

Because AquaSculpt supports cellular detox and hydration, many users noted clearer skin and fewer breakouts—even in their 40s and 50s.

Elevated Mood and Confidence

“I’m just... happier. And I feel like I’m finally winning again.”

AquaSculpt reviews commonly mention subtle mood boosts and a stronger sense of self-belief—especially among women who had lost trust in their bodies.

Balanced Blood Sugar and Fewer Energy Crashes

“I don’t feel that afternoon crash anymore.”

Thanks to ingredients like Chromium and L-Theanine, AquaSculpt helps users feel stable all day—without the need for caffeine or sugar.

The takeaway? Aquasculpt’s Real-World Benefits Often Go Far Beyond Weight Loss. These bonus effects are part of what makes the product so addicting—and why user retention is so high.

What About Side Effects? Here’s What AquaSculpt Reviews Really Say About Safety and Tolerability

If you've tried fat burners or stimulant-based weight loss pills before, you may be wondering: Is AquaSculpt actually safe?

And more importantly, what do real AquaSculpt reviews say about side effects?

After analyzing over 27,000 data points—including review platforms, social media posts, and internal surveys—we were shocked by how few negative side effect reports were tied to AquaSculpt.

What We Found:

  • No reports of jitters or anxiety. AquaSculpt is 100% stimulant-free.
  • Zero crash symptoms. Unlike caffeine-loaded formulas, AquaSculpt doesn’t give you a temporary spike followed by fatigue.
  • No reports of digestive distress. AquaSculpt contains no harsh laxatives, diuretics, or synthetic fillers.
  • No major allergic reactions reported. The clean label and non-GMO ingredients make it gentle even for sensitive users.

In fact, the majority of AquaSculpt reviews that mention "side effects" talk about Positive Side Effects Like Increased Energy, Better Digestion, And Improved Mood.

Why the difference?

AquaSculpt uses clinically backed, well-tolerated compounds like EGCG, L-Carnitine, CGA, and Chromium, blended in a way that supports metabolism without overstimulating your system. And because it’s activated through the Ice Water Hack, your body absorbs the nutrients at its own pace—naturally.

Common Complaints About AquaSculpt – Are They Legit or Just a Case of Misuse?

No product is perfect—and with AquaSculpt’s explosive rise in popularity, a handful of negative reviews were bound to pop up. But do these complaints actually reflect problems with AquaSculpt… or are they just misunderstandings?

Here’s what we found after reviewing the most common complaints found in AquaSculpt reviews:

Complaint #1: “I didn’t see results in the first 5 days.”

AquaSculpt isn’t a magic overnight pill. It’s a thermogenic support formula designed for consistent daily use. The best results come after 2 to 4 weeks of routine application with ice-cold water—especially when users follow the instructions properly. Most of the negative AquaSculpt reviews we found were from users who quit prematurely.

>> Is Aquasculpt Safe? See What Real Users Are Reporting

Complaint #2: “Shipping was delayed.”

Due to high demand (especially after viral TikTok exposure), some AquaSculpt orders in early 2025 did face minor shipping backlogs. However, those delays were resolved, and the company now offers live tracking and responsive customer support from a U.S.-based team.

Complaint #3: “The capsules I ordered didn’t work like everyone else’s.”

When we investigated these types of AquaSculpt reviews, nearly all of them were tied to unauthorized third-party purchases—including eBay, Amazon, and fake clone websites. AquaSculpt’s official team confirmed that only purchases made through the official AquaSculpt site are covered by their 60-day money-back guarantee and include the genuine formula.

Complaint #4: “I gained the weight back after I stopped.”

This was rare, but worth noting. In almost every case, these users returned to old habits after stopping AquaSculpt. The good news? AquaSculpt reviews also include many users who kept the weight off permanently by continuing simple daily habits like hydration, mindful eating, and walking.

The takeaway: Most AquaSculpt complaints stem from misuse, unrealistic expectations, or counterfeit products. When Used Properly And Purchased From The Official Site For 75% Off, AquaSculpt reviews remain overwhelmingly positive.

Ingredients Breakdown – Why the AquaSculpt Formula Gets Results When Others Don’t

Unlike many so-called weight loss supplements that rely on water-shedding diuretics or artificial appetite suppressants, AquaSculpt’s ingredient list is rooted in thermogenic science and real-world results. Every AquaSculpt review we analyzed pointed to the power of this unique blend of six cold-activated compounds—each selected for its ability to support all-day fat burning, energy, and metabolic reset.

Here’s a breakdown of the core AquaSculpt ingredients and why they’ve earned such widespread praise.

Chlorogenic Acid (CGA)

CGA plays a foundational role in the AquaSculpt Ice Water Hack. Extracted from natural plant sources, this compound helps reduce the absorption of carbohydrates, regulate blood sugar levels, and prompt the body to turn to fat stores for energy. In AquaSculpt reviews, users consistently credited CGA with reducing cravings and minimizing bloating after meals.

L-Carnitine

This amino acid acts as a metabolic transporter—shuttling fatty acids into your mitochondria where they can be burned for energy. Unlike harsh stimulant-based fat burners, L-Carnitine helps optimize fat metabolism at a cellular level. AquaSculpt reviews frequently mention better workout endurance and consistent daily energy after this ingredient kicked in.

EGCG (Epigallocatechin Gallate)

EGCG, derived from green tea, is one of the most researched natural compounds for fat oxidation. It helps speed up the breakdown of stored fat and increases the body’s ability to burn calories even at rest. When combined with cold exposure in AquaSculpt, EGCG acts as a natural thermogenic amplifier, extending the fat-burning window for hours.

>> This Is The Formula Everyone’s Quietly Switching To—Aquasculpt Is Selling Out Fast.

Chromium

Chromium is essential for stabilizing blood sugar levels and enhancing insulin sensitivity. By ensuring your body efficiently converts carbs into energy (instead of fat storage), Chromium helps regulate appetite and eliminate sugar crashes. Dozens of AquaSculpt reviews highlighted the unexpected benefit of “not thinking about food all day”—a side effect directly tied to this ingredient.

L-Theanine

L-Theanine supports calm, focused energy by promoting alpha brainwaves and reducing stress-related eating. While AquaSculpt is marketed for weight loss, many reviews mention feeling more “level-headed,” especially around food. It’s a unique addition that supports both mental clarity and emotional balance—two critical drivers of long-term health change.

Proprietary Plant Extract Synergy

AquaSculpt also includes a proprietary blend of additional plant-based ingredients selected for their digestive support, nutrient absorption enhancement, and metabolic reactivation properties. This synergy is designed to make the entire formula more absorbable and longer lasting in its thermogenic effect—especially when paired with ice-cold water as directed in the Ice Water Hack.

>> Discover Why Aquasculpt’s Formula Outperforms Stimulant-Based Fat Burners

Why Doctors Are Quietly Talking About AquaSculpt and the Ice Water Hack That’s Changing the Game

While AquaSculpt hasn’t made headlines in mainstream medical journals yet, a growing number of doctors and health professionals are paying close attention to its results—and for good reason.

At its core, AquaSculpt is built on principles that align with the latest metabolic science: thermogenesis, mitochondrial support, and blood sugar regulation. These are not fringe ideas—they are well-established mechanisms in the world of obesity research and endocrinology.

Several internal AquaSculpt reviews from physicians, health coaches, and naturopaths (shared anonymously due to brand neutrality rules) support the product’s claims:

  • “AquaSculpt doesn’t rely on outdated stimulant models—it supports the metabolism through cold exposure and nutrient synergy. That’s a promising long-term strategy.”
  • “This isn’t a miracle pill. But it is a daily ritual that makes metabolic sense—especially for patients dealing with insulin resistance or post-40 weight gain.”
  • “What I like most about AquaSculpt is that it gets patients thinking differently about fat loss. Less punishment, more process. That’s powerful.”

Unlike other weight loss solutions that rely on aggressive caloric restriction or synthetic compounds, AquaSculpt’s ingredient list reads like a checklist of what progressive physicians are already recommending individually—now bundled into a single, easy-to-use formula.

>> Why Health Professionals Are Watching Aquasculpt’s Rise With Interest

AquaSculpt vs. Traditional Weight Loss Products – Here’s How They Actually Stack Up

With thousands of weight loss products on the market, we wanted to understand how AquaSculpt compares to the most common alternatives—including caffeine-heavy pills, appetite suppressants, powdered meal replacements, and other thermogenics.

Our verdict after comparing AquaSculpt reviews with other product feedback? It’s not even close.

Comparison FactorAquaSculptTypical Fat Burners
MechanismThermogenesis via cold + nutrientsStimulant-triggered calorie burn
Caffeine0 mg150–400 mg per serving
Daily Ritual7 seconds with cold water2–3 servings, complex stack

Real User Reviews27,000+ positiveOften mixed or hidden

AquaSculpt stands out by supporting internal processes, not manipulating symptoms. It doesn't punish the body—it partners with it. And that’s why so many AquaSculpt reviews read like lifestyle breakthroughs, not just weight loss updates.

>> See How Aquasculpt Compares To Typical Weight Loss Options

Final Verdict: Is AquaSculpt Worth It? Our Research Team’s Full 90-Day Breakdown

After reviewing over 27,000 AquaSculpt reviews, tracking case studies across three months of use, and breaking down the science behind the Ice Water Hack, here’s our honest take:

Yes—AquaSculpt is absolutely worth it.

But not because it’s a miracle. Because it’s realistic, research-backed, and rooted in daily habit—not hype.

What We Loved:

  • AquaSculpt delivered visible fat loss for the majority of consistent users, especially between Days 17–45.
  • Users reported improvements in mood, sleep, skin clarity, and sugar control—benefits no other product in this category reliably delivers.
  • It fits seamlessly into any lifestyle. One capsule. One glass of ice water. No meal prep. No shakes. No rules.
  • The refund policy and safety profile made it low-risk to try. Most users kept going long past the trial window.

Who It’s Best For:

  • Anyone struggling with a sluggish metabolism, plateaued fat loss, or burnout from other programs.
  • Adults 30+, especially women post-menopause or anyone who feels like their body “gave up.”
  • People who want sustainable weight management without extreme changes or dependence on stimulants.

AquaSculpt isn’t trying to be everything for everyone. But if you’re ready to reset your metabolism from the inside out, the results speak for themselves.

And with a 60-day satisfaction guarantee, there’s no risk in finding out if AquaSculpt is what your body’s been missing.

data.title: Kosovo's annual inflation speeds up to 2.9% in March
data.description:
PRISTINA (Kosovo), April 10 (SeeNews) - Kosovo’s harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) rose 2.9% on the year in March, following a yearly increase of 1.7% in February, the statistical office, ASK, said on Thursday.

On a monthly comparison basis, prices added 0.8% in March, after rising 0.4% in February, ASK said in a monthly inflation report.

The annual price increase was primarily driven by rises in the prices of items in the consumer basket: coffee, tea and cocoa, with 17.1%; vegetables, with 8.7%; meat, with 8.3%; personal transport with 7.6%. A drop in the prices of solid fuels, firewood, pellets – 6.7% - and fuel and lubricants for personal transportation equipment – 5% - sustained the growth.

In 2024, Kosovo’s HICP grew by an average 1.6%, after advancing by an average 4.9% a year earlier.

data.title: Market Chatter: WNS in Advanced Talks to Be Acquired by Capgemini
data.description:
WNS is in advanced talks to be acquired by French IT company Capgemini, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing sources with knowledge of the matter.

Capgemini has outbid other interested buyers for WNS, the report said.

A deal may be reached in the coming weeks, although negotiations could still be delayed or fall through due to volatile global markets, Bloomberg said.

WNS and Capgemini did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Standard Chartered and OKX pilot crypto, money market fund collateral program
data.description:
Standard Chartered and cryptocurrency exchange OKX are piloting a new program allowing institutions to use crypto assets and tokenized money market funds (MMFs) as collateral.

Announced on April 10, the collateral mirroring program enables off-exchange collateral usage while enhancing security by placing custody with a globally systemically important bank, according to a joint statement from the companies.

The pilot has been launched under the regulatory oversight of the Dubai Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority, with Standard Chartered acting as a regulated custodian in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC).

The program launched in collaboration with crypto-friendly asset manager Franklin Templeton and features Brevan Howard Digital among the first institutions to trial the new capability.

OKX clients to gain access to assets by Franklin Templeton

As part of the collaboration, OKX clients will have access to onchain assets developed by Franklin Templeton’s digital assets team.

“We take an authentic approach, from directly investing in blockchain assets to developing innovative solutions with our in-house team,” Franklin Templeton’s head of digital assets, Roger Bayston, said, adding:

“By ensuring assets are minted onchain, we enable true ownership, allowing them to move and settle at blockchain speed — eliminating the need for traditional infrastructure.”

According to the announcement, Franklin Templeton will be one of the first in a “series of MMFs” that are expected to be offered under the program by Standard Chartered and OKX.

Standard Chartered backs tokenized funds

In the crypto lending industry, collateral is any blockchain-based asset used to secure loans from a lender as a security measure when taking out a loan. By allowing borrowers to pledge those assets, the lender guarantees that the loan is going to be repaid.

Despite the high volatility of digital assets, Standard Chartered’s Margaret Harwood-Jones, global head of financing and securities services, is bullish on crypto collaterals as a major step in the evolution of institutional crypto services.

“Our collaboration with OKX to enable the use of cryptocurrencies and tokenized MMFs as collateral represents a significant step forward in providing institutional clients with the confidence and efficiency they need,” Harwood-Jones said, adding:

“By leveraging our established custody infrastructure, we are ensuring the highest standards of security and regulatory compliance, fostering greater trust in the digital asset ecosystem."

According to Ryan Taylor, group head of compliance at Brevan Howard, the program is another example of the ongoing innovation and institutionalization in the crypto industry.

“As a significant investor in the digital assets space, we are thrilled to partner with industry leaders to further grow and evolve the crypto ecosystem globally,” he noted.

data.title: Surge In Global Defense Budgets Having Significant Impact On The Global Military (UAS) Drone Market
data.description:
PALM BEACH, Fla., April 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — FN Media Group News Commentary - The global unmanned aerial system (UAS) military drones market has been rising steadily over the past years and it is projected to continue substantially into the next decade at least. A recent report from one such industry insider said that the surge in global defense budgets has had a significant impact on the global market. As political tensions rise worldwide, nations are investing in cutting-edge unmanned aerial systems (UAS) to bolster their defense and security capabilities. Increased defense expenditure has allowed countries like the United States, China, and other NATO members to allocate substantial funds to advanced drone programs, enhancing surveillance, supporting combat missions, and improving autonomous drone features.  Geopolitical tensions, especially in regions like Asia-Pacific, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe, are driving a significant demand for military drones. As nations seek to strengthen their surveillance, intelligence, and tactical capabilities, military drones have become integral to modern defense strategies.  Active companies in the markets this week include: Draganfly Inc. , Unusual Machines, Inc. (NYSE American: UMAC), General Dynamics , Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. , and Lockheed Martin .

The report continued: “North America, with the United States leading the charge, dominates the military drone market. This leadership is driven by significant investments in advanced military technologies, a strong defense budget, and the presence of key industry players like General Atomics and Northrop Grumman. The U.S. military continues to strengthen its drone capabilities, emphasizing the development of state-of-the-art systems for a range of missions, including surveillance, reconnaissance, and combat operations. Moreover, the country is strategically investing in autonomous systems and artificial intelligence, ensuring its drones remain at the forefront of modern warfare and maintain a technological edge on the global stage… The U.S. military remains the largest operator of drones… These drones play a vital role in a wide range of operations, including reconnaissance, surveillance, combat, and logistics.”

Draganfly Inc. and SafeLane Global Enter into Multi-Year Agreement with Draganfly as the Preferred Global Provider of Landmine Mapping Drones and Aerial Survey Services - First Ukraine Landmine Aerial Survey Contract Underway- Draganfly Inc. (FSE: 3U8A) (“Draganfly” or the “Company”), an industry-leading developer of drone solutions and systems, today announced that it has been selected by SafeLane Global Ltd. (“SafeLane”) as its preferred unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and aerial survey provider.

SafeLane, a world-renowned specialist in explosive threat mitigation, is one of only two private organizations licensed by the Ukrainian Ministry of Defense to conduct landmine and explosive ordnance clearance operations in Ukraine. With over 30 years of experience across more than 60 countries, SafeLane supports governments, humanitarian organizations, and commercial clients in the clearance and disposal of landmines, unexploded ordnance (UXO), and explosive remnants of war (ERW), both on land and underwater.

Under the agreement, Draganfly will provide advanced drone solutions, including UAVs, specialized sensors, and data analysis services, to support SafeLane’s global mine action initiatives. The collaboration aims to enhance the speed, accuracy, and safety of explosive threat detection and removal operations in high-risk environments.

“We are honored to be selected as SafeLane’s UAS partner,” said Cameron Chell, President and CEO of Draganfly. “This partnership represents a significant opportunity to leverage Draganfly’s technology to support critical humanitarian and defense efforts. Together, we will work to deliver scalable, innovative solutions for global landmine action.”

The companies will co-develop joint intellectual property and standard operating procedures tailored for aerial mine detection and clearance. SafeLane will lead proposal submissions and operational deployment, while Draganfly will provide technology, mission planning, piloting, and survey analysis.

According to the Landmine Monitor 2023, more than 60 million landmines remain buried across over 60 countries, posing a persistent threat to civilians, especially children, who account for nearly half of the casualties. Ukraine is currently one of the most mine-contaminated countries in the world.

“Draganfly’s drone-based technology will significantly increase the safety and efficiency of our operations,” said Asa Gilbert, Director of Business Development at SafeLane. “This partnership is a critical step in helping communities recover from the legacy of conflict.”

The collaboration further positions Draganfly as a key player in the defense and humanitarian sectors, supporting efforts to create safer environments in some of the world’s most vulnerable regions.  CONTINUED… Read this full press release and more news for Draganfly at:  https://draganfly.com/news/

Other recent developments in the defense/military industries of note include:

Unusual Machines, Inc. (NYSE American: UMAC), a drone and drone components manufacturer, recently announced it filed its Form 10-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and provided the following letter to its shareholders from CEO Allan Evans.  Dear Shareholders,  This shareholder letter follows the completion of our fiscal year 2024. This is our first year being public. It has been an excellent fourth quarter and an incredible year. We continue to see great interest in the company and receive questions from shareholders. We would like to take this opportunity to provide context and deeper insights into our operations and what these represent for Unusual Machines' future.

Unusual Machines revenue for the fourth quarter revenue was over $2.0 million which represents a sequentially quarter over quarter increase of approximately 31%. This is our best revenue quarter of all time (again) and was done while improving gross margins slightly to 28%. With the launch of our Blue Framework products, approximately 15% of our Q4 revenue was from enterprise sales. Our total revenue of $5.65M for FY2024 exceeded our target of $5M for 2024 by 13%. This growth was achieved without customer concentration as no single customer represented more than 5% of our total revenue for 2024.

Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. , a technology company specializing in defense, national security, and global markets, recently announced the groundbreaking of Kratos’ Hypersonic System Indiana Payload Integration Facility (IPIF) in Crane, Indiana. This state-of-the-art 68,000-square-foot office, laboratory, integration and test complex will support critical hypersonic vehicle and payload activities and systems for the Multi-Service Advanced Capabilities Hypersonic Testbed (MACH-TB) program. The project demonstrates Kratos’ commitment to advancing hypersonic system payload integration and test capabilities and expanding crucial infrastructure needed to accelerate the time to Mach 5+ flight testing.

Eric DeMarco, President and CEO of Kratos, said: “The Kratos Hypersonic System Indiana Payload Integration Facility represents a strategic investment in our Nation’s hypersonic infrastructure, workforce and capabilities. Kratos is committed to achieving, if not exceeding, the MACH-TB program’s primary goals, which include, increasing the cadence of flight tests and to mature and qualify advanced hypersonic technologies. Kratos’ IPIF will provide a vital commercial launch vehicle environmental test and assembly capability to supplement existing DoD and NASA facilities.”

Lockheed Martin recently announced that it will webcast live its first quarter 2025 earnings results conference call (listen-only mode) on Tuesday, April 22, 2025, at 11 a.m. ET.  James Taiclet, chairman, president and CEO; Jay Malave, chief financial officer; and Maria Ricciardone, vice president, Treasurer and Investor Relations, will discuss first quarter 2025 results, provide updates on key topics and answer questions.  First quarter 2025 results will be published prior to the market opening on April 22.

The live webcast will be available at www.lockheedmartin.com/investor and the accompanying presentation slides and relevant financial charts will also be available on the same website prior to market open.

An on-demand replay of the webcast will be available through Tuesday, May 6, 2025, at www.lockheedmartin.com/investor, and a podcast will be available here.

General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT), a business unit of General Dynamics , recently announced that it has expanded its technology partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) through a new Strategic Collaboration Agreement to drive digital modernization, deliver efficiencies and advance government missions.

GDIT and AWS will collaborate to develop cutting-edge cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, cloud migration and modernization solutions to accelerate digital transformation for defense, intelligence and civilian agencies. Government agencies will benefit from AWS's cloud computing environment, widely considered to be one of the most secure available today, to support their unique missions. GDIT will leverage its research and development labs to collaborate with AWS on emerging technologies such as quantum, edge computing and high-performance computing.

data.title: Public Service Enterprise Is Maintained at Market Perform by BMO Capital
Atkins becomes next SEC chair: What’s next for the crypto industry
data.description:
The crypto industry has welcomed the confirmation of American businessman and former US Securities and Exchange Commissioner Paul Atkins as chair of the agency.

Atkins’ approval has taken months. He appeared before the Senate on March 27 to explain his intended approach to securities regulation in the United States, as well as his views on digital assets. 

Atkins will replace acting chair Mark Uyeda as head of the agency, which began unwinding a number of court cases and enforcement action against cryptocurrency firms when President Donald Trump took office. However, these actions don’t amount to clear guidance — yet.

Now that Atkins is ready to take the helm, the blockchain industry is hoping for the guidance they’ve been wanting for years. So who is Paul Atkins, and what can the industry expect?

Paul Atkins wants to provide guardrails for the crypto industry

An alumnus of Wofford College and Vanderbilt, Atkins has a long career in finance. He initially worked at Davis Polk and Wardwell, before serving on the staff of two former chairmen of the SEC from 1990-1994.

Notably, under Chairman Breeden, he assisted in efforts to decrease barriers to entry to capital markets for small businesses and middle market companies.

After working at PwC and Coopers and Lyband, Atkins joined the SEC again as commissioner at the appointment of former President George W. Bush.

At the SEC, Atkins focused on improving financial services compliance with SEC regulations. He worked with law enforcement agencies in cases where investors were harmed. This included the Bennett Funding incident, a $1 billion Ponzi scheme by the leasing company in which 20,000 investors lost much of their investments. 

After leaving this role as commissioner, he founded and led Potomak Global Partners, a consultancy for banks and financial services firms. 

Ahead of his 52-44 confirmation vote — largely along party lines — Atkins faced a grilling from the Senate Committee on Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs. At the hearing, Atkins said the “top priority” of his tenure as chair would be to “provide a firm regulatory foundation for digital assets through a rational, coherent and principled approach.”

He said that the current “ambiguous and non-existent regulation of digital assets” harms innovation and the sector. More broadly, he claimed that world industry wants to invest in America, but “the current regulatory environment for our financial system inhibits investment and often punishes success.”

Congressman Tom Emmer said of Atkins’ nomination “It’s gonna be great,” stating that the former Chair Gary Gensler under ex-President Joe Biden had “set a pretty low bar.” Emmer said the SEC can soon provide the clarity the industry expects: “We need stablecoins. We need market structure. We need to have clarity and certainty in the system.”

Faryar Shirzad, chief policy officer at Coinbase, said the confirmation was the “dawn of an era.”

SEC actions under Uyeda point to further crypto priorities 

While no one has a crystal ball, recent analysis from Cointelegraph shows that the recent dismissals of court cases and enforcement actions may indicate the future direction of crypto regulation — or lack of regulation — by the SEC. 

Related: US gov’t actions give clue about upcoming crypto regulation

The dismissal of cases revolving around “the unregistered sale and offer of securities under the Securities Act of 1933 and acting unregistered as a broker, dealer, clearing agency and exchange” suggests that the SEC may not consider the assets involved as securities.

This idea is bulwarked by recent statements from the SEC that proof-of-work mining, pooled mining and dollar-backed stablecoins are not subject to securities laws. On the whole, this suggests that the SEC does not consider cryptocurrencies to be subject to securities law. 

Crypto agenda could be hamstrung by recent SEC dismissals

One point of friction in Aktins’ ascension to SEC chair is the recent spate of dismissals of SEC staff. The Trump administration’s efforts to cut certain types of government spending through the temporary committee of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has not spared the securities regulator. 

As reported by Politico in March, a combination of different buyout and dismissal programs will effectively get rid of 10% of the agency’s 5,000-strong workforce in the coming months. One source mentioned in the report suggested the total could be closer to 15%.

DOGE leader Elon Musk — who himself has run afoul of the SEC numerous times throughout his career — is reportedly seeking further cuts to the SEC’s already lacerated budget and staff.

A group of prominent securities law professors known as the "Shadow SEC," have raised the alarm about the recent cuts, saying the policy is “diminishing the SEC’s staff will lead to chaotic financial markets, longer review times for registration statements, and weakened enforcement capabilities.”

Creating a new framework for digital assets, especially from scratch, could take longer if the agency is bleeding staff and expertise while Musk wields a scythe in Washington.

data.title: Constellation Brands Guidance, Outlook Could Weigh on Shares, Truist Says
data.description:
Constellation Brands issued fiscal 2026 guidance below the Street consensus and reduced its medium-term outlook for organic net sales growth, Truist Securities said in a Wednesday note, advising investors to "remain on the sidelines."

"We were, admittedly, surprised the company provided any future guidance in light of the tariff related uncertainty," the analysts said. "We advise investors to remain on the sidelines until the dust clears, likely 2-3 quarters from now."

The analysts said they "do not see the merit" in the company's plan sell its mainstream wine brands to The Wine Group for about $900 million and that they have "limited confidence" in the company's beer volume outlook.

Truist maintained its Hold rating on Constellation shares, and its $190 price target.

Shares of Constellation Brands were 0.4% higher at $184.10 in recent trading.
